data.title: Veritone sell media agency Veritone One to Insignia Capital
data.description:
• Veritone (NASDAQ:VERI) said it has sold its media agency, Veritone One, to Insignia Capital.
  • The firm added, the total consideration from the sale will be up to $104M, including $18M in cash dependent on Veritone One’s achievement of certain net revenue targets during calendar year 2025.
  • The net proceeds will be used to retire a significant portion of Veritone’s term debt and to fund future operations.

data.title: Nukkleus to implement 1-for-8 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Nukkleus (NASDAQ:NUKK) said it will implement a 1-for-8 reverse split of its stock on October 24.
  • The firm is implementing the split to regain compliance with the minimum bid price rule.

data.title: Norfolk Southern Non-GAAP EPS of $3.25 beats by $0.14, revenue of $3.1B beats by $10M
data.description:
• Norfolk Southern press release (NYSE:NSC): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.25 beats by $0.14.
  • Revenue of $3.1B (+3.3% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • On an adjusted basis, the operating ratio for third quarter 2024 was 63.4%. This represents 570 basis points of improvement from adjusted third quarter 2023 which was 69.1%.

data.description:
• PACCAR press release (NASDAQ:PCAR): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.85 beats by $0.03.

data.title: Interactive Brokers Group adds $78M to Q3 expenses for dispute settlement
data.description:
• Automated global electronic broker Interactive Brokers Group (NASDAQ:IBKR) said it will add $78M to its Q3 expenses for settlement of "certain disputes".
  • The stock was trading -0.45% Tuesday pre-market at $148.97.
  • The company reached agreements in principle "to settle certain disputes which relate back to events that took place prior to the end of the quarter".
  • IBKR filed a Form 8-K/A disclosing revised operating results for the quarter ended September 30 yesterday.
  • The company reported Q3 non-GAAP EPS of $1.75, misses by $0.07, and revenue of $1.37B (+20.2% Y/Y), beats by $30M.
  • IBKR has adjusted its Q3 non-GAAP EPS to $1.61 from $1.75. General and administrative expenses are revised to $153M from $75M.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures fall as investors exercise caution amid earnings season
data.description:
Stock index futures fell on Tuesday, while yields were slightly higher, as investors grew cautious amid the earnings season, and as the U.S. presidential election nears.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.4%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.5% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) were flat at 4.21%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.06%.

Wall Street's major averages closed out the session mixed on Monday, as they wait for significant earnings reports to be released throughout the week.

"Yesterday, equities struggled, particularly as bond yields kept on rising," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

Yesterday’s pontification of Federal Reserve speakers created a stir in bond markets. There were a range of views expressed, from “keep on cutting” to “more gradual cuts”. Markets, rendered skittish by Fed’s “data dependency” on undependable data, have raised yields, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"With two weeks to go today until the election, markets have started the week a bit more nervously than during the last six, where the S&P 500 has gone up each week for only the second time since the pandemic," Reid added.

On the earnings front, General Electric (GE), General Motors (GM), and Verizon Communications (VZ), among others, are expected to report before the bell on Tuesday.

On the economic calendar, the October Richmond Fed Manufacturing Index is expected today, and is anticipated to come in at -19.

data.title: Trump polls better on the economy than Harris, Reuters-IPSOS poll says
data.description:
More voters expect that Former President Donald Trump will be better for the economy than Vice President Kamala Harris in a recent Reuters/IPSOS poll, in which 46% favor the Republican candidate, while 38% prefer the Democratic one.

That contrasts with a recent AP-NORC poll, which showed Harris and Trump about even on the economy.

Some 60% of registered voters in the Reuters-IPSOS poll said the economy is heading in the wrong direction, and 70% said their cost of living was heading the wrong way.

The poll indicated that the economy and immigration, along with threats to democracy, were the country's most important problems. Besides the economy, Trump also led on immigration — 48% to 35%, Reuters reported.

On the national popular vote, when voters were asked who they prefer, Harris had a small edge over Trump — 46%-43%, in the poll conducted over six days, ending on Monday. Her lead is within the margin of error, with the poll showing Harris ahead only 2 percentage points when using unrounded figures.

The election, though, isn't determined by the popular vote but by the Electoral College and is likely to hinge on a handful of battleground states. From that view, Harris and Trump are neck-and-neck, Reuters said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Lockheed Martin reports mixed Q3 results; raises FY24 outlook
Old National Bancorp Non-GAAP EPS of $0.46 in-line, revenue of $485.86M beats by $3.66M
data.description:
• Old National Bancorp press release (NASDAQ:ONB): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.46 in-line.
  • Revenue of $485.86M (+6.5% Y/Y) beats by $3.66M.
  • Net interest income on a fully taxable equivalent basis of $397.9 million.
  • Net interest margin on a fully taxable equivalent basis of 3.32%, down 1 basis point.
  • Pre-provision net revenue of $219.7 million; adjusted PPNR of $229.3 million.
  • Efficiency ratio of 53.8%; adjusted efficiency ratio of 51.2%.
  • Provision for credit losses of $28.5 million.

data.title: RTX's stock rises with quarterly profit, higher earnings guidance
data.description:
RTX’s (NYSE:RTX) stock rose 2.2% in premarket trading Tuesday after the company swung to a profit as sales rose, and raised its guidance for the full year as its order backlog hit a record $221 billion.

The aerospace and defense company reported a third-quarter profit of $1.47 billion, or $1.09 a share, compared with a loss of $984 million, or $0.68 a share, a year earlier.

Excluding one-time items, adjusted earnings were $1.45 a share, beating the average estimate among Wall Street analysts of $1.34 a share.

Sales rose 6% to $20.09 billion, beating the consensus estimate of $19.84 billion.

Chief Operating Officer Chris Calio said demand, especially within its defense business and what’s known as the aftermarket for parts and service, was robust.

RTX (RTX) raised its adjusted sales guidance to between $79.25 billion and $79.75 billion for the full year. The company also expects adjusted earnings of $5.50 to $5.58 a share, up from prior guidance of as much as $5.45 a share.
